task_completion.description: Read the tooling design specification file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/tooling_design_spec.txt, showing the required dimensions.

task_completion.description: Open the U-channel profile in SolveSpace
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace GUI is open, displaying the 8-segment U-channel profile.

task_completion.description: Apply the overall width and leg height constraints
task_completion.visual_evidence: SolveSpace GUI showing a horizontal distance constraint of 120.0mm (overall width) and a vertical distance constraint of 70.0mm (leg height) applied to the sketch.

task_completion.description: Apply the inner clear width and depth constraints
task_completion.visual_evidence: SolveSpace GUI showing a horizontal distance constraint of 90.0mm (inner clear width) and a vertical distance constraint of 55.0mm (inner clear depth) applied to the inner segments of the sketch.
SolveSpace GUI showing a distance constraint of 15.0mm applied to represent the wall thickness of the U-channel.

task_completion.description: Save the fully constrained file to the target location
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or terminal command showing the file being saved exactly as /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/u_channel_constrained.slvs.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to apply the constraints
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the SolveSpace graphical interface to add dimensions. (Note: Using a text editor initially to fix the corrupt file is expected and allowed, but the actual constraining should ideally involve the GUI or valid CAD operations).

integrity.description: The agent saved the output to a new file rather than overwriting the input
integrity.visual_evidence: The original u_channel_profile.slvs should remain distinct from the newly created u_channel_constrained.slvs.
The final shape must still be a U-channel with 8 connected line segments, not a newly drawn arbitrary shape.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The specification requires the following constraints: overall channel width = 120.0mm, leg height = 70.0mm, wall thickness = 15.0mm, inner clear depth = 55.0mm, inner clear width = 90.0mm. CRITICAL NOTE: The initial input file 'u_channel_profile.slvs' contains a fatal bug (constraint type 82 instead of 81 for vertical lines) that causes SolveSpace to crash immediately upon opening. The agent MUST first open the .slvs file in a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) to change the '82's to '81's before it can be successfully opened in the SolveSpace GUI. Do not penalize the agent for using a text editor for this initial fix; it is a necessary step to overcome a bug in the environment.
